title: docs/tutorials: add a basic network config

comments.body: Should we tell them to install our CNI config instead? We have a good default living in the repo (in contrib/cni I think?)
sure? I'm trying to package cri-o, buildah, and podman for another distro, and files like the cni config make for messy deps, i feel.

I think you're safe to include the Podman one in the Podman package. It's set to be (fairly) low priority by default, so installing the CRI-O one shouldn't conflict with it. We explicitly request the network by name if `/etc/containers/libpod.conf` exists and is the default from this repo.
@vbatts Whats going on with this PR, do you agree with @mheon ?
updated to use the podman provided cni config. PTAL
